_publications._publicationTitle: Modelling neurofibromatosis type 1 tibial dysplasia and its treatment with lovastatin.

_contextSnippet: Consistent with the function of lovastatin as an indirect inhibitor of Ras prenylation, the bone pro-anabolic effect of lovastatin correlated with the normalisation of MAPK signalling measured as a phospho-Erk1/2 to Erk1 ratio
details: Successfully detected MAPK pathway activation status through pErk1/Erk1 ratio measurement in calvaria bones, showing normalization with lovastatin treatment
